@evt_001.Description: Devavrata born to King Shantanu and goddess Ganga, the eighth Vasu incarnate.
@evt_001.Outcome: Ganga returns to heaven after giving Devavrata to Shantanu
Consequences: Bhishma raised as crown prince of Hastinapura

@evt_002.Description: Devavrata takes terrible vow of lifelong celibacy so Shantanu can marry Satyavati.
@evt_002.Outcome: Shantanu marries Satyavati; Devavrata becomes Bhishma

@evt_003.Event_Name: Niyoga - Birth of Dhritarashtra, Pandu, Vidura
@evt_003.Description: Vyasa fathers Dhritarashtra (from Ambika), Pandu (from Ambalika), and Vidura (from maid) through Niyoga at Satyavati's request.
@evt_003.Outcome: Three sons born to continue Kuru line

@evt_004.Description: Pandu crowned king of Hastinapura as Dhritarashtra is blind.
@evt_004.Outcome: Pandu becomes king, conquers many kingdoms
Consequences: Dhritarashtra passed over due to blindness

@evt_005.Description: Pandu accidentally kills sage Kindama in deer form; cursed to die if he approaches a woman.
@evt_005.Outcome: Pandu retires to forest with Kunti and Madri
Consequences: Sons born through divine invocation instead

@evt_006.Description: Kunti invokes Surya using Durvasa's mantra; Karna born with divine armor (Kavacha-Kundala). Kunti abandons infant in river.
@evt_006.Outcome: Karna found and raised by charioteer Adhiratha and Radha
Consequences: Karna's identity unknown until before war

@evt_007.Description: Kunti invokes Yama (Dharma); Yudhishthira born as eldest Pandava.

@evt_008.Description: Kunti invokes Vayu; Bhima born with immense strength.

@evt_009.Description: Gandhari gives birth to a mass of flesh; Vyasa divides it into 101 pots. 100 sons and 1 daughter (Duhshala) born. Duryodhana is firstborn.
Consequences: Omens at Duryodhana's birth - jackals howled

@evt_010.Description: Kunti invokes Indra; Arjuna born as third Pandava.

@evt_011.Description: Madri invokes Ashvins using Kunti's mantra; twin sons Nakula and Sahadeva born.

@evt_012.Description: Pandu approaches Madri despite the curse and dies. Madri commits sati.
@evt_012.Outcome: Pandu and Madri die; Kunti brings Pandavas to Hastinapura

@evt_013.Description: Drona becomes teacher of Kuru princes. Arjuna proves best student.
Consequences: Rivalry between Pandavas and Kauravas deepens

@evt_014.Description: Ekalavya, self-taught archer, gives his thumb as guru-dakshina to Drona.
@evt_014.Outcome: Ekalavya can no longer shoot as well

@evt_015.Description: Kuru princes display skills. Karna arrives and challenges Arjuna. Duryodhana crowns Karna king of Anga.

@evt_016.Description: Duryodhana plots to burn Pandavas in house of lac at Varanavata. Vidura warns them; they escape through tunnel.
@evt_016.Outcome: Pandavas escape; world believes them dead

@evt_017.Description: While hiding at Ekachakra, Bhima kills the demon Bakasura terrorizing the village.

@evt_018.Description: Arjuna (disguised as Brahmin) strings the bow and wins Draupadi. Kunti says share; Draupadi marries all five Pandavas.

@evt_019.Description: Pandavas receive Khandavaprastha, clear land; Maya Danava builds magnificent palace of Indraprastha.

@evt_020.Description: Arjuna and Krishna help Agni burn Khandava forest. Arjuna receives Gandiva bow.
@evt_020.Outcome: Forest burned; Maya Danava saved; Gandiva obtained by Arjuna

@evt_021.Description: Yudhishthira performs Rajasuya Yajna and is crowned emperor. Shishupala killed by Krishna.
@evt_021.Outcome: Yudhishthira crowned emperor; Shishupala slain by Krishna's Sudarshana Chakra

@evt_022.Description: Before Rajasuya, Krishna, Arjuna, and Bhima go to Magadha. Bhima kills Jarasandha in wrestling duel.

@evt_023.Description: Shakuni defeats Yudhishthira in rigged dice game. Pandavas lose kingdom, wealth, Draupadi. Draupadi humiliated in court (vastraharan).
@evt_023.Outcome: Pandavas lose everything; Draupadi disrobed; Krishna saves her

@evt_024.Description: Called back for second dice game; Pandavas lose again. Sentenced to 12 years forest exile + 1 year incognito.

@evt_025.Description: Pandavas depart Hastinapura for 12-year forest exile. Citizens follow them.

@evt_026.Description: Arjuna travels to Himalayas, performs severe penance. Shiva (as Kirata hunter) grants Pashupatastra after combat.
Consequences: Arjuna receives other divine weapons from gods

@evt_027.Description: Arjuna taken to Indra's heaven. Trains under Indra and celestial warriors. Cursed by Urvashi (becomes eunuch for one year).
Consequences: Urvashi's curse useful during incognito year as Brihannala

@evt_028.Description: Jayadratha attempts to abduct Draupadi while Pandavas are away. Captured and humiliated by Bhima.
Consequences: Gets boon to hold 4 Pandavas (except Arjuna)

@evt_029.Description: While searching for Saugandhika lotus for Draupadi, Bhima meets his brother Hanuman in Gandhamadana.
@evt_029.Outcome: Hanuman blesses Bhima and promises to be on Arjuna's flag
Consequences: Hanuman present on Arjuna's chariot banner in war

@evt_030.Description: Sage Brihadarva narrates story of Nala and Damayanti to comfort Yudhishthira during exile.

@evt_031.Description: Duryodhana goes to forest to mock Pandavas; captured by Gandharva Chitrasena. Pandavas rescue him.

@evt_032.Description: Pandavas live incognito in King Virata's court: Yudhishthira as Brahmin Kanka, Bhima as cook Ballava, Arjuna as eunuch Brihannala, Nakula as horse-keeper, Sahadeva as cowherd, Draupadi as Sairandhri.

@evt_033.Description: Kichaka, Virata's commander, harasses Draupadi. Bhima kills him secretly.
Consequences: Suspicion arises that Pandavas are hidden in Virata

@evt_034.Description: Kauravas attack Virata to steal cattle and expose Pandavas. Arjuna (as Brihannala) single-handedly defeats entire Kaurava army.

@evt_035.Description: Krishna goes as peace ambassador to Hastinapura. Shows Vishvarupa. Duryodhana refuses to give even 5 villages.
Consequences: Karna learns his true identity from Kunti

@evt_036.Description: Kunti reveals to Karna that he is her firstborn. Asks him to join Pandavas. Karna refuses but promises he will not kill any Pandava except Arjuna.
Consequences: Karna promises Kunti she will still have 5 sons after war

@evt_037.Description: On the battlefield, Arjuna refuses to fight seeing kinsmen. Krishna delivers the Bhagavad Gita - discourse on duty, dharma, and paths to liberation.
Consequences: Most sacred text of Hinduism delivered

@evt_038.Description: Kurukshetra War begins. Bhishma is Kaurava commander. Pandava army led by Dhrishtadyumna.

@evt_039.Description: Arjuna uses Shikhandi as shield. Bhishma refuses to fight Shikhandi (former Amba). Arjuna pierces Bhishma with arrows. Bhishma falls on bed of arrows.
@evt_039.Outcome: Bhishma falls; lies on bed of arrows awaiting Uttarayana

@evt_040.Description: Abhimanyu enters Chakravyuha but cannot exit. Jayadratha blocks other Pandavas. Seven warriors kill Abhimanyu treacherously.
Consequences: Arjuna vows to kill Jayadratha before next sunset or self-immolate

@evt_041.Description: Arjuna fights through entire Kaurava army. Krishna creates artificial sunset. Jayadratha emerges; Krishna reveals sun. Arjuna beheads Jayadratha.

@evt_042.Event_Name: War Day 14 Night - Death of Ghatotkacha
@evt_042.Description: Ghatotkacha wreaks havoc on Kaurava army at night using powers. Karna uses Shakti weapon (meant for Arjuna) to kill Ghatotkacha.
Consequences: Karna loses his one-use weapon meant for Arjuna

@evt_043.Description: Ashvatthama elephant killed. Yudhishthira says 'Ashvatthama is dead' (the elephant). Drona lays down arms in grief. Dhrishtadyumna beheads Drona.

@evt_044.Description: Karna appointed Kaurava commander. Fierce battles continue.
Consequences: Shalya appointed as Karna's charioteer to demoralize him

@evt_045.Event_Name: War Day 17 - Karna Kills Duhshasana... Karna Killed by Arjuna
@evt_045.Description: Karna and Arjuna duel. Karna's chariot wheel sinks. While trying to lift it, Arjuna kills Karna on Krishna's urging. Earlier, Bhima kills Duhshasana and drinks his blood.
@evt_045.Outcome: Karna killed; Duhshasana killed by Bhima

@evt_046.Description: Shalya killed by Yudhishthira. Shakuni killed by Sahadeva. Duryodhana hides in lake. Called out; mace duel with Bhima. Bhima strikes below belt. Duryodhana falls.
@evt_046.Outcome: Duryodhana mortally wounded; war effectively ends
Consequences: Balarama furious at Bhima's foul blow

@evt_047.Description: Ashvatthama, Kripacharya, and Kritavarma attack sleeping Pandava camp at night. Ashvatthama kills all Upapandavas (Draupadi's five sons) and Dhrishtadyumna.
@evt_047.Outcome: Upapandavas, Dhrishtadyumna, and others killed in sleep
Consequences: Cursed by Krishna to wander 3000 years

@evt_048.Description: Ashvatthama launches Brahmashira astra at Pandavas. Arjuna counters. Vyasa intervenes. Ashvatthama redirects weapon at Uttaraa's womb. Krishna saves Parikshit. Ashvatthama cursed to wander 3000 years.
@evt_048.Outcome: Ashvatthama stripped of gem; cursed by Krishna

@evt_049.Description: Women of both sides mourn the dead. Gandhari curses Krishna that Yadavas will destroy themselves. Dhritarashtra tries to crush Bhima (Krishna substitutes iron statue).

@evt_050.Description: Bhishma, lying on bed of arrows, teaches Yudhishthira about dharma, governance, and philosophy over many days.
@evt_050.Outcome: Comprehensive teachings on kingship and dharma delivered

@evt_051.Description: On Uttarayana (sun's northward journey), Bhishma gives up his life. Body cremated on the banks of Ganga.

@evt_052.Description: Yudhishthira crowned king of Hastinapura. Rules with dharma.

@evt_053.Description: Yudhishthira performs Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice) to establish sovereignty. Arjuna follows the horse and fights various kings including his son Babhruvahana.

@evt_054.Event_Name: Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, Kunti Retire to Forest
@evt_054.Description: After 15 years of Pandava rule, Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, Kunti, Vidura, and Sanjaya retire to forest.

@evt_055.Description: Forest fire kills Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, and Kunti. Sanjaya survives.
@evt_055.Outcome: Three elders perish in forest fire

@evt_056.Description: Gandhari's curse fulfilled. Yadavas fight among themselves with iron pestles grown from eraka grass. Krishna's sons, Pradyumna, Samba die. Balarama dies. Krishna shot by hunter Jara.
@evt_056.Outcome: Yadava dynasty destroyed; Krishna dies; Dvaraka sinks into sea
Consequences: Arjuna fails to protect Yadava women

@evt_057.Description: Pandavas renounce kingdom, crown Parikshit. Walk towards Himalayas. One by one, Draupadi, Sahadeva, Nakula, Arjuna, Bhima fall. Only Yudhishthira reaches summit with a dog (Yama in disguise).
@evt_057.Outcome: All Pandavas except Yudhishthira fall on the journey

@evt_058.Description: Yudhishthira reaches heaven. Sees Duryodhana in heaven, Pandavas in hell (illusion). After test, all united in Svarga.
@evt_058.Outcome: Yudhishthira passes final test; all heroes united in heaven
